title: Retention cron entrypoint + non-blocking session-end hook
depends_on: T-110 (streaming readers, so retention rotates without breaking readers)
current_state: T-109 builds the retention engine but does not wire it into the runtime. Policies need to fire automatically â€” either via daily cron / Task Scheduler, or opportunistically at session end. Neither exists today.
structural_problem: An engine that nobody calls is dead code. Retention must run on a schedule to deliver value.
target_state: (a) scripts/retention_tick.py is a standalone Python script that calls run_all(DEFAULT_POLICIES) and exits with 0/1 based on errors. (b) Session-end protocol includes an opportunistic 'retention_tick' op that calls run_all with a 5-second time budget. Daemon thread so it cannot block session exit beyond the budget. (c) Documentation in PI.md Â§11 explains how to schedule the cron / Task Scheduler entry.
files_affected: agent/session_exit.py (or wherever exit ops are listed in pi_agent.py â€” add 'retention_tick' op)
PI.md (add section under Â§11 ops with cron/Task Scheduler example)
audit_refs: Phase 8.7 audit â€” wires #10-#14 #20 into runtime
design_notes: scripts/retention_tick.py: imports agent.retention.run_all + DEFAULT_POLICIES, calls run_all, prints summary, exits 0 on success / 1 on any error
Add --policies <name1,name2> filter to run a subset (useful for ad-hoc invocation)
Session-end op: define as agent/session_exit.py ops list entry: ('retention_tick', _exec_retention_tick, {'budget_s': 5.0})

_exec_retention_tick: spawn daemon thread that calls run_all; main session-exit waits up to budget_s for thread completion; if not done, log timeout via track_silent and continue exit (retention will run on next cron tick anyway)
Daemon thread design avoids blocking exit even if a policy hangs (e.g. SQLite locked)
Task Scheduler / cron suggestion in PI.md Â§11: daily at 03:00 local time, command: 'python E:\pi\scripts\retention_tick.py'
Lock file usage from T-109 ensures cron + session-end never collide
success_criteria: python scripts/retention_tick.py exits 0 on a healthy repo state
python scripts/retention_tick.py --dry-run reports policies but does not mutate files
Session exit completes within session_exit_budget_s + retention_budget_s (5s) max regardless of policy state
PI.md Â§11 contains a one-liner cron / Task Scheduler example
risk_notes: Risk: session exit hang if retention spawns a thread that holds a SQL lock. Mitigation: budget_s timeout + daemon thread (process exit kills it). Risk: cron and session-end racing. Mitigation: file lock from T-109. Risk: cron path differs Windows vs Unix â€” provide both examples in PI.md.
migration_plan: 1. Write scripts/retention_tick.py with argparse for --dry-run and --policies
2. Integrate into session exit: locate the exit ops list in pi_agent.py (R4 resumable exit work made this concrete); add a new op entry
3. Implement _exec_retention_tick helper: daemon thread + budgeted wait + timeout reporting
4. Document scheduling in PI.md Â§11 with both Windows Task Scheduler XML snippet (or schtasks /create command) and Unix cron example
5. Add testing/test_retention_tick.py: invoke the script as subprocess (or via runpy); assert exit code 0
6. Add testing/test_session_exit_retention.py: patch run_all to sleep > budget; assert session exit still completes within budget + slack
test_plan: test_retention_tick_exits_zero_on_clean_state â€” empty repo, no policies applicable; script returns 0
test_retention_tick_dry_run_no_mutations â€” populate files; run with --dry-run; assert mtimes unchanged
test_retention_tick_policies_filter â€” --policies turns_log,evolution_log; assert only those run
test_session_exit_retention_within_budget â€” patch policy to sleep 1s, budget 5s; assert exit completes in < 6s
test_session_exit_retention_timeout_logged â€” patch policy to sleep 30s, budget 2s; assert exit completes in ~2s; assert track_silent recorded 'retention.session_exit_timeout'
test_concurrent_invocations_safe â€” start script twice in parallel; both complete; file lock prevents corruption
